subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Pesticide Safety at Home
description: One-minute skill: Pesticide Safety at Home.

hook.text: The bug spray you use in the kitchen works by attacking the nervous system. Bugs' and yours.

buildup.text: Organophosphate pesticides kill insects by blocking acetylcholinesterase—a nerve enzyme.

discovery.text: Humans have the same enzyme. High exposure causes nausea, seizures, and respiratory failure.

twist.text: Children playing on treated lawns absorb pesticides through skin at rates adults wouldn't notice.

climax.text: Using targeted baits instead of sprays reduces exposure risk dramatically.

punchline.text: If it kills bugs by attacking nerves, your nerves aren't immune either.

quiz.question: How do organophosphate pesticides kill insects?
options: By blocking a nerve enzyme called acetylcholinesterase
